articles.title: UH OH. TIKTOK JUST MADE ITS DESKTOP EXPERIENCE WAY BETTER
articles.summary: TikTok's desktop update improves UX and adds an Explore tab, new streaming modes, and a floating player. While enhancements make browsing seamless, some users miss the old, clunkier version, which helped limit screen time. The changes bring the desktop experience closer to mobile, making TikTok even harder to resist.

articles.title: META'S CHANGED THE FACEBOOK MESSENGER LOGO AT THE WORST POSSIBLE TIME
articles.summary: Meta has reverted the Messenger logo to its original blue, undoing the gradient redesign introduced in 2020 without explanation. While some speculate it aligns with Meta's recent ideological shifts, others suggest it reflects the company's abandoned plan to merge Messenger with Instagram.

articles.title: OVER 1,700 ARTISTS AND ARTS WORKERS WILL RECEIVE AID FROM THE LA ARTS COMMUNITY FIRE RELIEF FUND
articles.summary: The LA Arts Community Fire Relief Fund, led by the J. Paul Getty Trust, alongside several art institutions in the city, has raised over $14 million to support 1,700 artists and arts workers affected by wildfires. Most recipients will receive up to $10,000. The Center for Cultural Innovation is administering the funds, prioritizing swift distribution and accessibility.

articles.title: GROWTH BEGINS WHERE COMFORT ENDS. YES, IT'S EASIER SAID THAN DONE
articles.summary: Growth isn't a linear journey, but a process of embracing discomfort, uncertainty, and bold risks. Designers unlock their full potential and drive meaningful transformation by stepping beyond their comfort zones, challenging fear, and developing soft skills.

articles.title: ACCESSIBLE DESIGN CHECKLIST
articles.summary: Penpot's Accessible Design Checklist plugin helps designers enhance inclusivity by reviewing layout, typography, color contrast, navigation, and interaction design. Inspired by WCAG, it provides a practical tool for accessibility improvements, recognizing that while perfect accessibility is unattainable, thoughtful design enhances usability for all.

articles.title: COLORMAXXING
articles.summary: Colormaxxing explores colors beyond sRGB using Display-P3 and Rec. 2020 color spaces. It features a color picker, compares wide-gamut colors, and explains how standard hex and RGB values limit vibrancy, offering insights on maximizing color depth in browsers.

articles.title: USER-INTERFACE ELEMENTS: GLOSSARY
articles.summary: A glossary to help designers quickly clarify definitions for key graphical user-interface elements and controls.

articles.title: BLUR EVERYTHING BUT YOUR ACTIVE WINDOW
articles.summary: Monocle is a sleek, minimalist app that removes distractions by blurring everything but your active window, giving you the focus you need.

articles.title: CHARACTER BUILDER
articles.summary: Browse through thousands of character images created by AI or create your own using the top AI models.

articles.title: MATTERNITY'S BOLD TAKE ON BRANDING AND VISUAL IDENTITY: UNVEILED!
articles.summary: Matternity's branding by GRUND – Creative Studio combines boldness with refinement to challenge traditional consulting. Featuring a minimalist logo, contemporary typography, and modern color-blocking, the design emphasizes clarity, honesty, and social impact.

articles.title: UX IS DEAD, LONG LIVE UX
articles.summary: Traditional UX focusing on product interfaces is becoming less valuable. Businesses must shift to journey-centric design, orchestrating customer experiences across multiple touchpoints. AI enhances this approach but requires an updated UX strategy.

articles.title: IN DEFENSE OF TEXT LABELS
articles.summary: Icons alone rarely communicate meaning effectively, often increasing cognitive load and slowing navigation. Text labels improve clarity, reduce ambiguity, and enhance efficiency, particularly in complex interfaces. Combining icons with labels strengthens usability by balancing visual hierarchy with clear communication.

articles.title: BEST PRACTICES FOR COGNITIVE ACCESSIBILITY IN WEB DESIGN
articles.summary: This guide covers WCAG 2.2 guidelines, design patterns, and practical solutions to enhance clarity, navigation, and interaction for diverse cognitive needs.

articles.title: HERE'S WHAT WE CAN LEARN FROM STARBUCKS' COMPOSTABLE CUP DESIGN FAIL
articles.summary: Starbucks' compostable cups were criticized for spills and their opaque design, raising questions about balancing sustainability and functionality in packaging.

articles.title: ROBOT FOOD REFRESHES CARLSBERG'S CRAFT BEER BRAND JACOBSEN
articles.summary: Robot Food updated Jacobsen Craft Beer's identity with bolder patterns, refined branding, and improved packaging to balance heritage with everyday premium appeal.
